context.meal: buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing
context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop in Front

episode_text:
day=7; meal=buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing; setting=Working on laptop with laptop in Front; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=1 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=fast; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or another cold-served item THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user selected no microwave for a fruit meal, consistent with preserving intended cold serving temperature. IF the meal is a warm savory item in a front-oriented work context THEN 1 min or 3 min depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both 3 min and 1 min for warm savory meals while working on a laptop in front, showing that reheating preference varies within that context rather than always favoring maximum heat. IF the meal is a hot savory item in a front-facing social context THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user previously selected a shorter reheating time for a warm meal in that context. Otherwise, observed warm-meal evidence supports moderate reheating rather than a fixed extreme.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected front-occlusion minimization in front-social and laptop-in-front contexts. IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity to the right of the user THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected right-occlusion minimization in right-oriented contexts.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves a social partner or valued activity directly in front of the user THEN medium or fast depending on the meal/context BECAUSE the user previously selected medium in front-oriented contexts and has now also selected fast while working on a laptop in front, indicating a front-context tendency toward quicker motion than the quiet-setting default. IF dining context involves a social partner or valued activity to the right of the user THEN medium BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected medium in right-oriented contexts. No evidence supports a stable use of fast outside the front work context.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food acquisition must remain compatible with limited mouth opening THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and has consistently selected the narrower-bite option.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF the user's physical constraints are relevant to the meal context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, and observed selections consistently favor the mode that does not require leaning forward.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside-mouth transfer. IF the user's physical constraints are considered for food delivery THEN not applicable BECAUSE the observed preference and physical profile support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF initiating feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging.

IF initiating drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging.

IF initiating mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF completing feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected the reliable perception-based option and this avoids requiring extra physical input.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF completing drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based drinking completion less suitable.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF completing mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based wiping completion less suitable.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose retraction in front-oriented contexts, including both front-social and laptop-in-front meals, consistent with wanting to reduce prolonged visual obstruction of the main focus. IF dining context includes a social partner to the right of the user THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retraction in a right-social context, indicating that social presence on the right is sufficient to favor reducing obstruction. IF dining context involves a focal activity to the right without a social partner THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to retract while watching TV on the right, suggesting that right-sided visual activities alone do not trigger a preference for added retraction time.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social THEN less BECAUSE the user chose reduced dipping with sauce in a social context, suggesting a preference to limit messiness around others. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves a front-focused activity such as working on a laptop THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected less dipping for sauce-containing meals while working on a laptop in front, indicating a stable tendency to reduce messiness when attention is on a valued front activity. IF food does not include a dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant and the user selected no dipping for non-dippable meals.
